mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in falling unemployment periods are not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, it signals aggressive monetary accommodation that lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows, boosting stock valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rank IC over trigger periods is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with accommodative monetary policy create a favorable demand environment that lifts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS growth and negative FEDFUNDS change has rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year decline in housing starts signals a weakening real estate sector and broader economic slowdown, reducing corporate profits and investor confidence, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances triggered by HOUST yoy decline >5%, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady expansion in industrial production indicates rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC of INDPRO monthly change versus 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, markets anticipate tighter monetary policy from the Fed, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL yoy > 3%, or average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in both nonfarm payrolls and retail sales signals a synchronized expansion in labor income and consumer spending, creating a Goldilocks environment that pushes stock prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when both monthly changes are positive, or the joint signal's precision rate for positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5%, the economy is at full employment, which often precedes wage inflation and Fed tightening, putting downward pressure on equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE < 5.0%, or the average return during such periods is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10).
